questions.title: Baseline command
questions.context: Old parser package.json exposes pnpm run parse -> tsx src/start.ts and pnpm run parse:dist -> node dist/start.js. Current old repo commit is 3392ca2 and working tree is clean.

options.label: Source command as canonical
options.description: Use pnpm run parse as the primary parity baseline because it executes the TypeScript entrypoint directly and matches current development behavior.

options.label: Compiled command as canonical
options.description: Use pnpm run parse:dist as the primary baseline because it resembles production binary execution, with build output pinned before the run.

options.label: Record both
options.description: Run or document both commands, designate one canonical baseline, and record any command-level differences as Phase 1 evidence.

questions.title: Runtime pinning
questions.context: Local runtime check found Node v22.16.0 and pnpm 10.26.1, while old package.json declares packageManager pnpm@10.33.0. Phase 1 requires command, commit, runtime versions, env inputs, worker count, logs, and outputs.

options.label: Record observed local runtime
options.description: Capture the current local Node/pnpm versions and mark deviations from packageManager as baseline facts without enforcing a toolchain yet.

options.label: Require exact package manager
options.description: Planner should include steps to run or install the declared pnpm version before considering the old-parser baseline reproducible.

options.label: Create reproducible manifest
options.description: Record commit, Node, pnpm, lockfile hash, env vars, command, worker count, log path, and output hashes in a versioned baseline manifest.

questions.title: Worker count baseline
questions.context: Old runtimeConfig clamps WORKER_COUNT to 1..64 and defaults to CPU count minus one. Worker count affects old parser runtime and logs, even if outputs should be stable.

options.label: Use legacy default
options.description: Let the old parser choose CPU count minus one so the baseline mirrors normal historical operation.

options.label: Pin WORKER_COUNT=1
options.description: Prefer the most reproducible single-worker baseline first, then separately measure the legacy default for performance.

options.label: Pin two profiles
options.description: Capture a deterministic profile with WORKER_COUNT=1 and a benchmark profile using the legacy default or explicit production-like worker count.

questions.title: Baseline run scope
questions.context: ~/sg_stats contains 3,938 raw replay files totaling about 6.0G and results totaling about 142M. A full legacy parse may be expensive; Phase 1 still needs reproducible baseline evidence.

options.label: Inventory first
options.description: Only document commands, inputs, existing results, and hashes in Phase 1; defer expensive full old-parser execution to the parity/benchmark phase.

options.label: Curated subset run
options.description: Run the old parser or equivalent harness on selected representative replays to prove command viability without full-corpus cost.

options.label: Full baseline run
options.description: Phase 1 should execute the old parser on the full available corpus and capture logs/output hashes as the authoritative baseline.

questions.title: Manifest depth
questions.context: Roadmap Phase 1 requires a corpus manifest for ~/sg_stats/raw_replays, ~/sg_stats/results, and ~/sg_stats/lists/replaysList.json. Local facts: 3,938 raw files, 8,843 result files, and replaysList.json has 23,140 records.

options.label: Counts and sizes
options.description: Create a lightweight manifest with directory counts, byte sizes, timestamps, and notable files; fast and easy to keep committed.

options.label: Per-file checksums
options.description: Create a full per-file manifest with paths, byte sizes, hashes, and replay-list linkage for reliable drift detection.

options.label: Schema profiler
options.description: Include top-level keys, event/entity shape summaries, largest files, malformed files, and game-type distribution so Phase 3 has input-shape evidence.

questions.title: Fixture selection
questions.context: Research calls for representative normal, malformed, partial, old-format, winner-present, winner-missing, vehicle-kill, teamkill, and commander-side cases where available.

options.label: Representative matrix
options.description: Select fixtures by game type, date era, map/world, file size, event count, and known legacy edge cases.

options.label: Edge cases first
options.description: Prioritize hard cases such as teamkills, vehicle kills, null killers, duplicate names, connected-player backfill, missing winner, and commander hints.

options.label: Full corpus first
options.description: Profile every replay first, then choose fixtures based on actual distribution and anomaly reports.

questions.title: Historical results role
questions.context: ~/sg_stats/results contains existing published outputs for sg, mace, and sm. Project docs say the archive is for tests/golden validation, not production import.

options.label: Immutable golden inputs
options.description: Treat current results as historical golden evidence and compare against them without rewriting during Phase 1.

options.label: Regenerated old outputs
options.description: Prefer outputs regenerated from the pinned old parser command, using current results only as a cross-check.

options.label: Dual evidence
options.description: Record both current published results and regenerated old-parser outputs, then classify any differences explicitly.

questions.chat_more: User decision: annual/yearly statistics are a separate legacy entity, not regular aggregate results, and can be implemented later in v2. The legacy repo has src/!yearStatistics and ~/sg_stats/year_results with unique New Year-style nominations that do not exist in normal player/squad/rotation statistics. Phase 1 should document this separate yearly-statistics surface as historical reference material and keep it out of the v1 ordinary-stats scope.

questions.title: Large artifact policy
questions.context: Raw replay data is about 6.0G. Planning docs should stay reviewable; AGENTS requires clean git status after completed sessions.

options.label: Commit summary only
options.description: Commit compact manifests and instructions; keep raw data and bulky generated reports local or ignored.

options.label: Commit deterministic indexes
options.description: Commit machine-readable per-file indexes if they are compact enough, while excluding raw corpus and heavy outputs.

options.label: Local artifacts only
options.description: Do not commit corpus-derived files in Phase 1; document commands that regenerate them on machines with ~/sg_stats.

questions.title: Filter ownership
questions.context: Old getReplays filters by mission_name prefix sg/mace/sm, excludes sgs, applies an sm date cutoff after 2023-01-01 by month, and Phase 1 must document old filters, skip rules, and config inputs.

options.label: Harness owns filters
options.description: Treat old game-type filters and skip rules as legacy comparison behavior owned by the parity harness, not by the parser core contract.

options.label: Parser emits metadata
options.description: Parser core should emit enough metadata for filters, while harness/server decide which replays count for compatibility and public stats.

options.label: Preserve in compatibility projection
options.description: Include legacy filter decisions as named compatibility rules in parser aggregate projections for old-vs-new comparisons.

questions.title: Identity compatibility
questions.context: Old parser uses entity IDs, connected events, same-name merging, getPlayerName, getPlayerId, excludePlayers.json, and ~/sg_stats/config/nameChanges.csv. New parser must preserve observed identity only; canonical matching belongs to server-2.

options.label: Compatibility only
options.description: Document old identity/name-change behavior only as a legacy aggregate compatibility layer, never as normalized identity truth.

options.label: Strict old aggregates
options.description: Require old name-change and same-name behavior to be reproduced exactly in aggregate projections while raw events keep observed identities.

options.label: Document before deciding
options.description: Phase 1 should map each old identity rule and defer exact preservation choices to contract/core planning.

questions.title: Legacy bug policy
questions.context: Research warns against treating the old parser as either absolute truth or disposable legacy. Phase 1 must define mismatch categories including old bug preserved, old bug fixed, new bug, insufficient data, and human review.

options.label: Preserve by default
options.description: Any comparable old behavior is preserved unless Phase 1 evidence names it as an intentional future change.

options.label: Fix with evidence
options.description: Allow the planner to mark obvious old bugs as candidates for fixing when source/corpus evidence and server/web impact are documented.

options.label: Human review gate
options.description: All suspected old bugs become human-review items; no preservation or fix decision is locked without explicit user approval.

questions.title: Legacy field map
questions.context: Old types include kills, killsFromVehicle, vehicleKills, teamkills, deaths, kdRatio, killsFromVehicleCoef, totalPlayedGames, killed/killer and teamkill relationship lists. Phase 4 will implement event semantics, but Phase 1 can map fields.

options.label: Map core stats now
options.description: Map the known player/global fields needed for initial parity and leave rare output folders for later discovery.

options.label: Map all old outputs
options.description: Phase 1 should inventory every published result file/folder and field before Phase 2 contract planning starts.

options.label: Map by roadmap phase
options.description: Phase 1 maps only legacy baseline fields; each later phase extends the map when it owns that behavior.

questions.title: Mismatch taxonomy shape
questions.context: Roadmap success requires any old-vs-new difference to be classified. server-2 will later persist parser artifacts, and web only sees data through server-2 APIs.

options.label: Standard categories
options.description: Use the planned categories as-is: compatible, intentional change, old bug preserved, old bug fixed, new bug, insufficient data, and human review.

options.label: Add severity and owner
options.description: Extend each mismatch with severity, owner app, affected field, replay evidence, and required follow-up before it can be accepted.

options.label: Add server/web impact
options.description: Classify mismatches by whether they affect parser-only artifacts, server persistence/recalculation, or UI-visible public stats.

questions.title: Cross-app depth
questions.context: Phase 1 includes product-wide compatibility rules. Local briefs state server-2 owns PostgreSQL, canonical identity, APIs, parse orchestration, and bounty calculation; web owns UI and generated OpenAPI types.

options.label: Briefs are enough
options.description: For Phase 1 baseline docs, rely on this repo's planning docs and gsd-briefs; deeper adjacent repo inspection waits for contract or worker phases.

options.label: Inspect adjacent repos
options.description: Have the planner include checks against server-2 and web repositories now, even though Phase 1 is mostly parser baseline documentation.

options.label: Create interface notes
options.description: Produce a small handoff note listing which Phase 1 discoveries can affect server-2/web later, without changing adjacent apps now.

questions.title: Phase 1 docs shape
questions.context: README and planning docs are current; no Rust workspace exists yet. Phase 1 needs downstream agents to find the baseline, corpus manifest, filters/skip rules, and mismatch taxonomy without re-discovery.

options.label: One dossier
options.description: Create one legacy-baseline dossier markdown that links to generated manifests and captures commands, rules, corpus facts, and taxonomy.

options.label: Separate docs
options.description: Create focused files for baseline command, corpus manifest, legacy rules, and mismatch taxonomy so each can evolve independently.

options.label: Plan artifacts only
options.description: Keep Phase 1 decisions in GSD PLAN/SUMMARY files first, and only promote stable docs after implementation produces evidence.

questions.title: Planning strictness
questions.context: Project requires 100% reachable-code coverage later, clean git handoff every completed session, and pushback on risky instructions. Phase 1 itself is documentation and local discovery work.

options.label: Evidence-gated docs
options.description: Require every Phase 1 claim to cite a local command, file path, or observed corpus fact before the plan can mark it done.

options.label: Pragmatic notes
options.description: Allow some Phase 1 notes to be marked as assumptions when command execution would be too expensive or risky, with clear follow-up tasks.

options.label: Strict verification
options.description: Plan must include verification commands for every deliverable and fail the phase if any baseline/corpus claim cannot be reproduced locally.
